hawkish_phrases: unit labour costs have continued to grow at rates above those consistent with meeting the inflation target in the medium term
some modest tightening of policy may be needed to maintain inflation sustainably at the target
a margin of excess demand builds thereafter
inflation rises slightly above it by the end of the forecast period
dovish_phrases: Policy may need to reinforce the expected recovery in UK GDP growth
CPI inflation fell to 1.3% in December, core CPI inflation declined to 1.4%
there has been a somewhat greater margin of spare capacity in the economy over recent years
should indicators of domestic prices remain relatively weak
CPI inflation is projected to remain below the MPC's 2% target throughout this year and much of 2021
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.75%, with guidance indicating policy may need to reinforce recovery if positive signals don't sustain, but also noting modest tightening may be needed further ahead if the economy recovers as projected.
